sectionOrderRules: Hero must convey calm and competence; avoid stock-business imagery and avoid showing surgical or distressing visuals on the first scroll.
Credentials must be visible above the fold on desktop so the visitor sees a licensed clinician, not just a brand.
Contact-cta should be a low-friction 'Boka tid' affordance, not a generic 'Kontakta oss'.
Treatment list must use plain language, not jargon — 'tandimplantat' rather than 'osseointegrated dental fixtures'.
Each treatment should give the visitor enough information to decide whether to book — typical duration, what happens during the visit, and indicative price band where the clinic publishes one.
Contact-cta at the bottom should reinforce the booking path that the page exists to drive.
Story must be specific (founder's name, year established, clinical philosophy), not generic 'we care about our patients' copy.
Team must list at least one named clinician with their credential or licence so the visitor recognises a regulated practitioner.
Phone number must be a tel: link and email a mailto: link.
Opening hours must list every day and mark closed days unambiguously — a visitor in pain on a Sunday should immediately see whether they can call.
Address must be readable as a single block so it can be copied straight into a maps app.
